<!--
This template is for content-only pull requests — knowledge packs, quiz banks,
weeks, STAR prompts, mock exercises, rubrics, documentation.

To use, append ?template=content.md to the PR creation URL, for example:
  https://github.com/aiforge-labs/platform-prep-kit/compare/main...BRANCH?quick_pull=1&template=content.md

For code PRs, open a PR normally — the default template applies.
-->

## Summary

What this PR adds or changes, and why. 1–3 sentences.

## Content changes

<!-- List each file or directory touched and the purpose. -->

- 
- 
- 

## Type of content

- [ ] New transition pack (e.g., `packs/xxx-to-yyy/` directory scaffold)
- [ ] New week / exercise / rubric within an existing pack
- [ ] New knowledge pack (`knowledge_packs/`)
- [ ] New quiz bank (`quiz_banks/`)
- [ ] New project template (`project_templates/` or `templates/`)
- [ ] Update to existing content (corrections, expansions, source refresh)
- [ ] Structural / navigation update (`plan.md`, `links.md`, README, index files)

## Sourcing

- [ ] All external sources cited are publicly accessible (no paywall for required reading)
- [ ] All source licenses are permissive (CC-BY, Apache 2.0, MIT, public domain) — or material is summarized and linked rather than reproduced
- [ ] No copy-paste from copyrighted sources; summaries link to the original
- [ ] No content drawn from employer or customer work, internal documents, or non-public discussions
- [ ] Paid books / courses / reports referenced without quoting or reproducing content
- [ ] External URLs checked; none returns 404

## Conventions

- [ ] New standalone files open with a **"Terms used"** callout expanding acronyms on first use
- [ ] Acronyms expanded on first use across the file (`SRE (Site Reliability Engineer)` etc.)
- [ ] In-repo links use relative paths (`../README.md`, `../../knowledge_packs/...`)
- [ ] Week / exercise structure follows the established pattern — Read / Apply / Articulate / Self-assess (for weeks) or equivalent for other content types
- [ ] No AI-slop: content has a specific point of view, cites concrete sources, and reads as if written by a practitioner

## Privacy & safety

- [ ] No secrets, API keys, tokens, or credentials in the diff
- [ ] No personal data (real names outside public-figure citations, email addresses, phone numbers, etc.)
- [ ] No customer names, customer-specific architectures, or internal system names
- [ ] No screenshots of internal tools or dashboards

## Review notes

<!-- Optional — what should the reviewer focus on? Anything deferred to a follow-up? -->
